channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Very cold weather is coming. US about to get 10th and chilliest polar vortex this winter
item.description: The coldest burst of Arctic air this season is coming to put an icy exclamation point on America's winter of repeated polar vortex invasions, meteorologists warn. And it will stay frozen there all next week.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas death row inmate mouths final 2-word message to victims' families before execution
item.description: A remorseful death row inmate pleaded for forgiveness and mouthed one final message before being put to death in Texas on Thursday, 20 years after he killed his strip club manager and another man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida newlyweds get justice after family fishing trip ended in murder
item.description: A Florida killer found guilty of killing a newlywed couple in front of their toddler was finally executed on Thursday, nearly three decades after his gruesome crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Karen Read and Ana Walshe: Love-tangled murder cases intertwine with state trooper linked to sultry texts
item.description: The Massachusetts trooper behind a series of unprofessional text messages in one Boston murder case is now seeing his credibility attacked in another.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Valentine’s Day poison plot that left husband of children's book author dead fueled by greed: docs
item.description: Kouri Richins, a Utah mother of three accused of killing her husband, previously tried to poison him twice before his death, including once on Valentine's Day, court records say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KTLA 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Vice President Vance takes in 'unspeakable evil' of Dachau
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday that her government wouldn't rule out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas executes man for 2004 double killing in a dispute with his strip club manager
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man who killed his strip club manager and another man, then later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled onto death row to threaten a lawmaker, was executed Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private border wall in South Texas still standing despite fraud by group fundraisers
item.description: Border Report revisited a privately built 3.5-mile segment of border wall south of Mission, Texas, after Steve Bannon pleaded guilty for fraud in a crowdsourcing scheme that raised millions of dollars for the project but paid very little for the building of the wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man is executed for the 1997 killing of a couple whose toddler witnessed the attack
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a husband and wife at a remote farm in an attack witnessed by the couple’s toddler was put to death Thursday in the state's first execution of the year. James Dennis Ford, 64, was pronounced dead at 6:19 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge pauses President Trump's order restricting gender-affirming care for trans youth
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s recent executive order aimed at restricting gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ax-wielding woman attacks daycare building on Walnut Grove
item.description: There were 68 children inside at the time. No one was injured, but the woman caused an estimated $8,000 in damages, police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman sues city over skeleton Christmas decorations
item.description: Alexis Luttrell put a skeleton and a skeleton dog in her yard in October for Halloween and later dressed them up for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, KRON4

item.title: Scarlett Johansson calls out AI celeb video slamming Kanye West
item.description: Actress Scarlett Johansson is calling for urgent AI safety legislation after a deepfake video of her and other Jewish celebrities went viral for slamming antisemitism by Ye, formerly Kanye West.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google's online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stars flee Kennedy Center groups after Trump seizes chair
item.description: President Donald Trump’s ascension to chair of the Kennedy Center on Wednesday led to multiple departures from the Washington, D.C., institution.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: At least 28 are hurt as driver plows into a demonstration in Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring at least 28 people including children, authorities said. Authorities said it was believed to be an attack, and the suspect — an Afghan asylum-seeker — was arrested.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois data shows inmates with violent records from shuttered prison sent to medium-security sites
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Among the approximately 400 inmates transferred when Illinois' decrepit Stateville prison closed over the summer, 278 were convicted of murder and 100 more are serving time for other violent offenses.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, KRON4

item.title: List: The more than 500 JOANN stores expected to close amid second bankruptcy filing
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Government watchdogs fired by Trump sue his administration and ask a judge to reinstate them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight government watchdogs have sued over their mass firing that removed oversight of President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
